ingredients.item: 2 large eggplants (1 to 11/3 pounds, or 500 to 600 g, each)
ingredients.original: 2 large eggplants (1 to 11/3 pounds, or 500 to 600 g, each)

ingredients.item: 3 to 4 tablespoons fresh lemon juice, or more to taste
ingredients.original: 3 to 4 tablespoons fresh lemon juice, or more to taste

ingredients.item:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
¼ cup (35 g) pine nuts
Olive oil
ingredients.original:
FOR GARNISHING AND SERVING
¼ cup (35 g) pine nuts
Olive oil

ingredients.item: 1 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C). Line a large baking sheet with aluminum foil.
ingredients.original: 1 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C). Line a large baking sheet with aluminum foil.

ingredients.item: 2 Pierce the eggplants with a knife to create 4 or 5 slits all over; this helps the steam to escape.
ingredients.original: 2 Pierce the eggplants with a knife to create 4 or 5 slits all over; this helps the steam to escape.

ingredients.item: 3 If you have a gas stove, use tongs to char each eggplant’s skin all over on an open flame, rotating them carefully for a few minutes until the skin is blistered. If you have an electric or induction stove, you can char the eggplants’ skin in a hot cast-iron skillet without any oil. Rotate the eggplants for a few minutes until the skin is blistered, then proceed to baking.
ingredients.original: 3 If you have a gas stove, use tongs to char each eggplant’s skin all over on an open flame, rotating them carefully for a few minutes until the skin is blistered. If you have an electric or induction stove, you can char the eggplants’ skin in a hot cast-iron skillet without any oil. Rotate the eggplants for a few minutes until the skin is blistered, then proceed to baking.

ingredients.item: 4 Place the eggplants on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 45 minutes, or until they are soft and can be easily pierced with a fork.
ingredients.original: 4 Place the eggplants on the prepared baking sheet and bake for 45 minutes, or until they are soft and can be easily pierced with a fork.

ingredients.item: 5 Cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh using a fork or spoon. Place the flesh in a fine-mesh strainer over a small bowl and allow it to drain its liquid for a few minutes.
ingredients.original: 5 Cut the eggplants in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh using a fork or spoon. Place the flesh in a fine-mesh strainer over a small bowl and allow it to drain its liquid for a few minutes.

ingredients.item: 6 Transfer the drained flesh to a medium bowl with the tahini, salt, pepper, garlic, and lemon juice. Mash it using a fork; this will create a chunky consistency. (If you like it smooth, you can pulse it in a food processor or use an immersion blender.) Adjust the seasoning to your liking.
ingredients.original: 6 Transfer the drained flesh to a medium bowl with the tahini, salt, pepper, garlic, and lemon juice. Mash it using a fork; this will create a chunky consistency. (If you like it smooth, you can pulse it in a food processor or use an immersion blender.) Adjust the seasoning to your liking.

ingredients.item: 7 Toast the pine nuts in a small, dry skillet over medium heat, for 5 minutes, or until golden, stirring continuously.
ingredients.original: 7 Toast the pine nuts in a small, dry skillet over medium heat, for 5 minutes, or until golden, stirring continuously.

ingredients.item: 8 Serve the baba ghanoush topped with oil, toasted pine nuts, pomegranate arils (if using), and parsley (if using).
ingredients.original: 8 Serve the baba ghanoush topped with oil, toasted pine nuts, pomegranate arils (if using), and parsley (if using).
